fixture.description: A class field declared with a definite-assignment assertion is never initialized (no constructor or initializer assigns it), so the first method that uses it throws a TypeError at runtime. The `!` suppresses the compiler, so static typecheck does not catch it.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that PlaylistView.items is declared with a definite-assignment assertion (`items!: string[]`) but is never assigned (no constructor or field initializer), so addItem's `this.items.push(...)` (and count()) throw a TypeError ('Cannot read properties of undefined') at runtime — and notes that the `!` is what hides this from the compiler. It should recommend initializing the field (e.g. `= []`). Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the uninitialized-field runtime crash is not surfaced.

expected_behavior: Bugs agent flags the never-initialized field behind a definite-assignment assertion
Notes the runtime TypeError on first use
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT assume some unseen constructor initializes it (no constructor exists in the file)
Does NOT treat this as a pure typing-style nit
